candidates.title: Village return as retail's shadow real estate
candidates.shift: Depopulated rural villages in the Global South and Europe are being reactivated through community-tourism schemes rather than economic 'return to industry' — repopulation via visitors, not residents.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: if abandoned settlements become semi-permanent tourist/hybrid-living nodes, they create a new peripheral real-estate class — neither store nor home — that mass retail has no format for yet.
candidates.evidence: Community-based tourism regeneration project in abandoned villages, southern Italy.

candidates.title: Living-algae concrete as CO2-eating structure
candidates.shift: Researchers in Peru are microencapsulating cyanobacteria (Nostoc) into concrete for self-healing and biological carbon capture, turning building fabric into a slow-metabolizing organism.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: if structural material becomes biologically active, the store/building shifts from inert asset to living infrastructure needing 'care' — a category retail facilities management doesn't have.
candidates.evidence: Microencapsulated Nostoc sphaericum in concrete for self-healing and CO2 capture, Andean Peru context.

candidates.title: Undocumented indigenous germplasm as supply-chain fragility
candidates.shift: Panama's cacao supply depends on indigenous-managed germplasm and propagation knowledge that is largely undocumented outside oral/community practice.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: mass retail's confectionery/food supply chains sit atop unmapped, undocumented biological knowledge systems — a hidden single point of failure with no formal registry or backup.
candidates.evidence: Study of undocumented cacao germplasm and indigenous propagation practices in Panama.

candidates.title: Basement wetness as inherited climate liability
candidates.shift: Changing precipitation patterns are intensifying chronic basement wetness and indoor microbial/radon hazards in disinvested urban neighborhoods, a slow health crisis tied to building substructure.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: as climate patterns shift, ground-level and below-grade retail/store real estate in older urban cores may accrue invisible liability — a category of building risk with no current insurance or code language.
candidates.evidence: Community-engaged study links basement wetness, microbial/radon exposure and precipitation change to health outcomes in disinvested neighborhoods.

candidates.title: Sahel's urban heat outpacing planning categories
candidates.shift: Rapid urbanization in Nigeria's Sahel zone is producing extreme land-surface-temperature hotspots that current urban resilience planning frameworks don't yet address.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: if semi-arid African cities become primary sites of new mass-retail buildout, thermal design standards imported from temperate markets may already be obsolete before construction finishes.
candidates.evidence: LST and thermal hotspot mapping in rapidly urbanizing Upper Benue River Basin, Nigeria.
candidates.source: Advances in Earth and Environmental Science

candidates.title: Governance vacuum for the 'global commons' as future real estate frontier
candidates.shift: Scholars propose democratic governance models for areas beyond national jurisdiction — deep seabed, Antarctica, outer space, atmosphere — currently ungoverned as property or territory.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: if seabed and orbital space become buildable/claimable within decades, retail and logistics real estate could extend into jurisdiction-free zones with no zoning doctrine at all — pure absence.
candidates.evidence: Paper proposes democratic governance framework recasting global commons (oceans, seabed, Antarctica, space, atmosphere).
